title: SELECT Queries 101

theory.introduction: The SELECT statement is the most commonly used SQL command. It allows you to retrieve data from one or more tables in a database.

sections.title: Selecting Specific Columns
sections.content:
Instead of retrieving all columns with *, you can specify exactly which columns you want. This is more efficient and makes your queries clearer.

SELECT name, population, continent
FROM countries;

sections.title: Using DISTINCT
sections.content:
The DISTINCT keyword removes duplicate values from your results. This is useful when you want to see unique values in a column.

SELECT DISTINCT continent
FROM countries;

This query will return each continent only once, even if multiple countries are from the same continent.

sections.title: Column Aliases (AS)
sections.content:
You can use the AS keyword to give a column a temporary name (alias) in your results. This is helpful for making output more readable.

SELECT name AS country_name, 
       population AS total_people
FROM countries;

sections.title: Arithmetic in SELECT
sections.content:
You can perform calculations directly in your SELECT statement:

SELECT name, gnp, population,
       gnp / population AS gnp_per_capita
FROM countries;

examples.title: Select specific columns
examples.description: Get country names and their continents

examples.title: Unique continents
examples.description: See all unique continents in the database

examples.title: Using aliases
examples.code: SELECT name AS Country, population AS Population FROM countries;

exercise.instruction: Write a query that selects only the name and email columns from the customers table in the e-commerce database.
exercise.starterCode: -- Select name and email from customers
exercise.hint: List the column names separated by commas after SELECT, then specify the table after FROM.

learningObjectives: Select specific columns from a table

quiz.question: Which keyword would you use to remove duplicate values from a query result?
quiz.explanation: DISTINCT is the SQL keyword used to return only unique (different) values in a result set.
